key_insight: The paper shows that adding retrieval, error tracing, and counterexample guidance can improve LLM-assisted theorem proving for formally verifying blockchain consensus protocols.
engineering_relevance: This makes formal verification of complex distributed systems more accessible and efficient, especially for security-critical blockchain consensus implementations.
method: Built an enhanced IsabeLLM pipeline for Isabelle theorem proving
Added Retrieval-Augmented Generation to supply more relevant proof context
Integrated error tracing, counterexample generation, and updated Isabelle/Sledgehammer compatibility
evidence_signal: The approach was evaluated by comparing the original and improved IsabeLLM on completing the formal verification of Bitcoin’s Proof of Work consensus.
watch_item: Results are tied to one consensus case study, so broader reliability across other protocols and proof domains still needs validation.
paper_title: IsabeLLM: Automated Theorem Proving Applied to Formally Verifying Consensus
